verdict_reasoning: STIM1 is a single-pass type-I transmembrane protein predominantly residing in the ER membrane, where it senses luminal Ca2+ and oligomerizes upon store depletion to activate ORAI channels at ER-PM junctions. A small but documented pool of STIM1 also reaches the plasma membrane surface, with its N-terminal domain (EF-hand) facing the extracellular space. This PM surface pool is real but minor compared to the ER-resident population, qualifying as dual localization. Antibodies targeting the extracellular EF-hand domain have been shown to bind live cells, confirming surface accessibility, but this is not the dominant localization.
